notes: The AKG C414 XLII is a large-diaphragm multipattern condenser microphone. It has a single physical connector: a 3-pin XLR output (balanced, pin 2 hot). Phantom power (48V nominal, 44–52 VDC accepted, ~4–4.5 mA) is delivered over the XLR signal cable per IEC 61938 — there is no separate power port or inlet. No rack mount; handheld/stand-mount form factor. The phantom power delivery via XLR is not modeled as a separate port because it shares the same physical connector as the audio output.

ports.name: Output
ports.note: Balanced XLR-3 output, pin 2 hot. Phantom power (44–52 VDC, ~4–4.5 mA per IEC 61938) is supplied to the mic over this same cable — there is no separate power connector.
